# Backlog

*Generated by `/jim:backlog` — 2026-04-24*

---

### `/jim:complete` skill

Extract `/jim:build`'s completion gate (ARCHITECTURE.md refresh, BACKLOG.md regeneration, plan-status confirmation) into a dedicated, re-invokable skill. Today the gate runs only as the tail of a long build run, where attention is thinnest. A standalone `/jim:complete` enables re-running completion work after interrupted builds, manual commits, or post-hoc drift, and gives future completion concerns (changelog updates, doc-site regen, notifications) a clean home. Idempotency is a design constraint — running the skill twice must be safe (regenerate not append, merge not duplicate). Config adherence motivated the extraction; the architectural separation of concerns is the stronger long-term reason.

**Sources:** `docs/specs/jim/012-config-adherence/spec.md`, `docs/brainstorms/20260422-config-adherence.md`

### `jim_path` shell helper

Ship a small helper — script or plugin-provided tool — that prints the resolved value of a config key for use in Bash invocations. `test -f "$(jim_path architecture)"` replaces hardcoded literal paths in shell calls. Native tool calls (Write, Edit, Read, Glob) cannot shell-substitute, so prose-level placeholders remain load-bearing; the helper hardens the Bash surface specifically. Largest investment of the deferred items — pays off across every future skill that references configurable paths.

**Sources:** `docs/specs/jim/012-config-adherence/spec.md`, `docs/brainstorms/20260422-config-adherence.md`

### Self-test meta-skill

Build a meta-skill that exercises every jim skill against a fixture project with non-default config and asserts no literal default filename ever leaks into a filesystem call. Catches the exact bug class that motivated 012-config-adherence and keeps catching it as skills evolve. Strongest long-term investment — the forcing function for all the other config-adherence improvements. Specific enforcement requirements (preamble invocation, schema value constraints) are detailed as ad-hoc items below — they should become acceptance criteria when this skill is specced.

**Sources:** `docs/specs/jim/012-config-adherence/spec.md`, `docs/brainstorms/20260422-config-adherence.md`

### Resolved-paths audit log

Persistent log file (e.g., `.jim/logs/resolved-paths.jsonl`) capturing each skill invocation's resolved-paths table for post-hoc debugging of which config was in effect when. Trivial to add once the resolve-paths preamble exists. Helps diagnose past drift — "which config produced this artifact?" becomes answerable from a file rather than reconstructed from the conversation log.

**Sources:** `docs/specs/jim/012-config-adherence/spec.md`, `docs/brainstorms/20260422-config-adherence.md`

### Cross-framework integration

Integration with other coding agents — Codex, Gemini CLI, and similar — so jim's SDLC discipline can travel beyond Claude Code. Currently jim is Claude-Code-only by design, but the pattern (specs, plans, research, agents) is portable. Larger investment than the other items; appropriate post-2.0 release.

**Sources:** `ROADMAP.md` (`## Later`)

### Research collaboration improvements

Refine the research workflow based on real-world usage feedback. Specifics emerge from actual user signals after the public release; held in backlog until concrete patterns surface.

**Sources:** `ROADMAP.md` (`## Later`)

## Ad-hoc

<!-- Items added here are preserved across /jim:backlog runs.     -->
<!-- Use `### Title` headings followed by a free-form description. -->
<!-- Add items manually or via `/jim:backlog add <description>`.   -->

### Self-test meta-skill enforces preamble invocation

Self-test meta-skill must verify every skill references `skills/_shared/resolve-paths.md` as its config-resolution entry point, preventing future skills from silently bypassing schema validation. Source: docs/specs/jim/012-config-adherence/security.md finding 5.

### Self-test meta-skill enforces schema value constraints

Self-test meta-skill must verify that every new `path.*` key added to `skills/_shared/config-schema.md` carries value constraints (relative path, no `..`, no leading `/`, resolved within project root), preventing silent weakening of validation over time. Source: docs/specs/jim/012-config-adherence/security.md finding 4.

### Configurable build hooks (per-task and completion gate)

Make build-time hook scripts configurable via .jim/config.md. Today /jim:build hardcodes `./pre-commit.sh` as the per-task quality gate, and the completion gate runs no extra script. Two new config keys would address this: (a) a per-task hook script — default `./pre-commit.sh` for backward compatibility, configurable to `make test`, `ci`, etc., or empty to disable; (b) a completion-gate hook script (e.g. `./ci.sh`) for a fuller test run that's too expensive per task — empty by default. Both validated via the schema. Source: dogfood feedback during 012-config-adherence — pre-commit.sh absent in test project caused build failure.

### Shellcheck CI for executable artifacts

`bin/jim_path` (introduced in spec 013) is jim's first non-markdown executable artifact. Add a CI workflow that runs `shellcheck bin/*` on every push to enforce sustained static-analysis discipline as the executable surface grows. Optional companion: a small test harness exercising each schema key against a fixture project — likely subsumed by the future Self-test meta-skill rather than built standalone. Source: docs/specs/jim/013-jim-path-helper/security.md finding 5.

---

## Themes

### Config adherence v2

The next-layer hardening of what 012-config-adherence started: a shell helper for the Bash surface, a self-test meta-skill that prevents future drift, and a persistent audit log for post-hoc debugging. Each addresses a specific gap that 012's prose-level placeholders + preamble pattern leaves open.

**Related items:** `jim_path` shell helper, Self-test meta-skill, Resolved-paths audit log, Self-test meta-skill enforces preamble invocation, Self-test meta-skill enforces schema value constraints

### Workflow extension

Deepening the SDLC surface beyond the current skills. Splitting `/jim:complete` out of `/jim:build` is the immediate move; refining research collaboration based on real-world signals is the longer-term one.

**Related items:** `/jim:complete` skill, Research collaboration improvements
